page:
# Day 8: The Deep Sea
## Focus
Hydrothermal vents, chemosynthesis, abyssal life, pressure adaptations, and bioluminescence.
## Learning Objectives
- Distinguish deep-sea ecosystems from sunlit marine habitats. - Explain chemosynthesis as an alternative energy pathway. - Identify common structural and behavioral adaptations to deep-sea conditions.
## Core Topics
### Hydrothermal Vents
Vent systems support communities based on chemical energy rather than sunlight. Symbiotic microbes allow many vent organisms to thrive in chemically extreme environments.
### Abyssal Plain Communities
Life in the deep sea often depends on sparse organic inputs from the surface, making efficiency and specialization important.
### Bioluminescence and Gigantism
Some deep-sea organisms use light for communication, camouflage, or predation. Others exhibit unusual body forms or large size associated with cold, high-pressure environments.
## Applied Activity
Write a short field-note style observation describing one imagined deep-sea organism. Include:
- habitat - feeding strategy - pressure or light adaptation
## Exit Question
What does the deep sea reveal about the range of conditions under which life can persist?
